help_subtitle: ADN SelfCare – Frequently asked questions and setup guide.
help_guide_desc: Step-by-step instructions on adn.systems. Video tutorial (Spanish) from NetDigital Venezuela.
help_step1_title: Click on the "Selfcare" menu on the top navigation bar.
help_img_step1: Selfcare menu and Access / Set / Recover Password button
help_step2_title: Click on the button "Access / Set / Recover Password".
help_img_step2: Form: callsign, email, Verify Identity button
help_step3_title: Enter your callsign and email address registered on RadioID.net, and click "Verify Identity". You will receive a link in your email to access the selfcare panel.
help_step3_caption: You will receive a link in your email. In the selfcare panel:
help_step4_title: If you have more than one ID registered to your callsign, select the ID for which you want to set the hotspot password.
help_step4_email: You will receive a confirmation email with the new password you must use to connect your hotspots.
help_step5_title: Configure your hotspot to use your new password
help_step5a_li4: Type your new password in the "Password" field.
help_step5a_li6: Reboot hotspot if connection does not work.
help_step5b_li2: Admin → Advanced → Quick Editors → DMR → DMR Gateway.
help_step5b_li4: Type your new password in the "Password" field.
help_step5b_li6: Reboot hotspot if connection does not work.
help_img_pista: Password field in DMR Network (Pi-Star / WPSD)
help_step_done: Your new secure password will be ready to connect successfully to the ADN Systems DMR network.
help_faq0_a: ADN SelfCare lets you create and manage your "DMR-ID secure" password. This password protects your DMR ID when connecting to ADN Systems (repeaters, hotspots, bridges). Use the SelfCare link to set it.
help_faq4_q: Is the DMR-ID secure password optional?
help_faq4_a1: Yes. It is optional. Users can choose whether to create it or not. If you do not create it, nothing changes. If you do create it, you must then use it in every client or device you use to connect to ADN Systems.
help_faq5_q: Where do I need to enter the password?
help_faq5_a1: If you create a DMR-ID secure password, you must enter it in all the software or devices you use to connect to ADN Systems. For example:
help_faq5_a2: At present, many of these programs do not yet have a dedicated field for this password. However, there are ways to enter it in most of them. Efforts are being made so that in the near future this option is visible in the main clients.
help_faq6_q: My client has no field for the password. What can I do?
help_faq6_a1: Many programs do not yet have a dedicated field for the DMR-ID secure password. There are workarounds to enter it in most of them. Efforts are underway so that in the near future this option is visible in the main clients.
console_desc: Start/end of call messages (GROUP VOICE START/END).
ss_intro: Here you can manage your hotspot preferences and static talk groups.
ss_calc_hint: Need help building your options line? Use the
active_qso_none: No active QSO at the moment.
tbl_filter_tg_hint: Shows all TGs whose number contains this (e.g. 730 = Chile)
tbl_filter_name_hint: Shows TGs whose name contains this text
sslog_manage_options_tg: Self Service lets you manage your device's Options line and static talk groups.
sslog_instruc: In order to manage your device with Self Service, you may need to remove all options in your device's software and set your password using PASS=your_password.
toptg_no_data: No TG count data yet. Data is saved when voice QSOs end (duration > 5s).
